rows: Touch and pressure sensation in left lower limb diminished but present

stem: A 25-year-old female is brought to the Emergency Department after a fall during a rock-climbing activity which caused severe right ankle pain and an inability to bear any weight on it. After examination and radiologic investigation she is diagnosed with a lateral malleolus fracture and casting is done before she is discharged. Out of self-care advice listed below, which points are relevant for this client? Mark them on the list below.
rationale: Self-care instructions with a cast include the following: Monitor the color and temperature of the toes of the right foot Do not insert any extra padding inside the cast Do not use any implement to scratch inside the cast If the edges of the cast get ragged visit the physician so that he or she can trim them Move toes frequently to prevent swelling of the lower limb Report to the physician in case of excess itching or any drainage from within the cast Cover the cast with a plastic bag while taking a bath to keep it from getting wet Category: Musculoskeletal, trauma medicine, multiple response question
options: Monitor the color and temperature of the toes of the right foot
Insert extra cotton padding inside the cast if it starts thinning out
Do not use any implement to scratch inside the cast
If the edges of the cast get ragged trim them to a length of no more than 1 inch with scissors
Move toes frequently to prevent swelling of the lower limb
Report to the physician in case of excess itching or any drainage from within the cast
Cover the cast with a plastic bag while taking a bath to keep it from getting wet

stem: A 68-year-old female has been admitted to the surgical ICU after a total hip replacement following a fracture. The registered nurse will position the client in which of the following ways in order to prevent dislocation of the prosthesis?
rationale: To prevent misalignment or displacement of the prosthesis the hip should be kept in a slightly abducted position using an abduction pillow which keeps the legs slightly apart. Category: Musculoskeletal system, post-operative care, multiple choice question
options: Maintain the hip in an adducted position
Maintain the hip in a slightly flexed and adducted position
The hip should be kept in an abducted position
The hip should be kept at 20 degrees of extension
The hip should stay internally rotated

stem: A newborn male has been diagnosed with developmental dysplasia of the hip and has been advised to be kept in a Pavlik harness for correction of the disorder. In the table below, mark the self-care points that the registered nurse will advise the client’s parents to follow.
rationale: Self-care advice to the client’s parents will consist of the following: Wear a diaper under the splint Check the color and movement of the toes daily Keep the hips abducted and externally rotated The splint should be worn continuously Check the skin under the straps 3 times per day Use a football hold when breastfeeding Hold and cuddle the infant as much as possible Category: Musculoskeletal system, pediatrics, grid question
rows: Wear a diaper under the splint
Check the color and movement of the toes daily
The splint should be worn for 6 to 12 hours a day
Check the skin under the straps 3 times per day
Use a football hold when breastfeeding
Do not hold the infant longer than necessary to prevent displacement of the straps

stem: A 58-year-old female has been diagnosed with bilateral osteoarthritis of the knee joints after the appropriate history, examination and radiological workup. Out of the nursing interventions listed below, which one is most important in order to prevent further wear and tear on the joints?
rationale: A client with osteoarthritis should be taught ergonomic principles and how to maintain correct alignment of the body in order to prevent further wear and tear and stress on the affected joints. Category: Musculoskeletal system, preventive medicine, multiple choice question Questions 192 and 193 are based on the case study given below Category: Musculoskeletal system, preventive medicine, geriatrics, case study
options: Teach the client how to maintain proper body alignment
Take Vitamin D and calcium supplements regularly
Take Vitamin C supplements regularly
Take as much bed rest as possible
Start performing bodyweight exercises

stem: A 68-year-old female has visited the family medicine clinic for her annual checkup. She complains that she has been experiencing attacks of dizziness on and off for the past week along with some episodes of blacking out. She also complains of a ringing sound in both ears sometimes. Her medical record shows that she was diagnosed with osteoporosis after a bone scan one month ago. The registered nurse is most concerned that this client is at risk of a _________________ due to her _________________.
rationale: The presence of osteoporosis places this client at an increased risk of fractures, with a fracture of the femur neck being most common. Given the client’s complaints of dizziness and blackouts she is classified as having a fall risk which in turn increases her chances of sustaining a femur fracture.
options: Femur fracture; increased fall risk
Humerus fracture; increased fall risk
Road traffic accident; hearing problems
Cerebrovascular accident; increased dizziness
Deep vein thrombosis; osteoporosis

stem: In the table below, mark which self-care measures will be advised to this client.
rationale: Self-care measures for a client with osteoporosis include the following: Encourage weight bearing and resistance exercises to help improve bone density Take a diet high in calcium Take a diet high in protein Supplemental calcium of 1200 mg per day Supplemental Vitamin D 800 IU per day Install walking aids around the house to prevent falls
rows: Take a diet high in calcium
Take a diet high in protein
Supplemental calcium of 2000 mg per day
Supplemental Vitamin D 5000 IU per day
Install walking aids around the house to prevent falls

stem: A 45-year-old diabetic male has been scheduled for a bone scan to visualize the extent of spread of his chronic osteomyelitis. The registered nurse in the orthopedic ward is preparing him for the procedure. Out of the following precautions, which ones should be taken for this client? Mark them on the list below.
rationale: The following preparatory steps are followed: No fasting is required Obtain signed informed consent Inject the radioisotope 2 hours before the procedure Drink plenty of water until the procedure takes place Empty the bladder before the scan is performed Category: Musculoskeletal system, radiology, multiple response question Questions 195 to 197 are based on the case study given below Category: Musculoskeletal system, pediatrics, case study A 2-year-old female is brought to the Emergency Department by her parents with the complaint of a suspected fracture of the right forearm after a fall down the stairs. Her medical record shows that she has been admitted three times in the past year with the complaint of fractures of various long bones. Her mother also complains that she has started limping while walking for the past 6 months. On general physical examination it is noticed that the client has a blue tinge to her sclera and that she does not respond very well to commands. She is of normal height and weight for her age and seems comfortable with her parents. If she needs anything, she points and indicates what she needs.
options: Fasting of 12 hours is required
Obtain signed informed consent
Inject the radioisotope 4 hours before the procedure
Drink plenty of water until the procedure takes place
Empty the bladder before the scan is performed

stem: In the scenario above, identify the top 4 cues which require following up with regards to the diagnosis and drag and drop them in the table below.
rationale: See rationale.
instruction: Top 4 cues

stem: Once casting has been done for the humerus fracture and analgesics administered the client is examined from head to toe. There are no other bruises or fractures currently and an eye examination shows no retinal hemorrhages. Out of the interventions listed below which one should be undertaken first for this client?
rationale: Since the client in this scenario does not have retinal hemorrhages, there are no other signs of abuse and she is comfortable around her parents child abuse is unlikely. The history of multiple fractures, presence of a limp and blue sclera make osteogenesis imperfecta the most likely diagnosis.
options: Call child protective services for the client
Order a full body bone scan
Refer for screening of Osteogenesis imperfecta
Order calcium and vitamin D levels
Admit the client for monitoring overnight

stem: After the appropriate workup the client is diagnosed with Osteogenesis imperfecta. In the table below, mark the nursing interventions which are indicated for this client.
rationale: Nursing interventions for a client with osteogenesis imperfecta consist of the following: Teach appropriate positioning to parents to avoid contractures Maintain good dental hygiene for the client as dental malformations are common in osteogenesis imperfecta Handle the client gently while examining to prevent fractures Refer the client for physical therapy for improved muscle strength Advise home modifications for increased safety for the client
rows: Teach appropriate positioning to parents to avoid contractures
Avoid brushing teeth too often to prevent teeth from falling out
Handle the client gently while examining
Refer the client for physical therapy for improved muscle strength
Refer the client for orthotic support for the legs
Advise home modifications for increased safety for the client

stem: An 18-year-old female with scoliosis has undergone spinal fusion surgery due to cardiopulmonary complications secondary to spinal curvature. After the surgery she is shifted to the surgical ICU. On the list below, highlight which nursing interventions are appropriate for this client.
rationale: Post-op care for a client with spinal fusion due to scoliosis consists of the following: Use a patient controlled pump to administer analgesic Assess bowel sounds regularly Monitor vital signs Encourage isometric exercises Monitor urine output after catheter removal Monitor neurovascular status of the limbs Category: Musculoskeletal system, post-operative care, multiple response question
tokens: Use a patient controlled pump to administer analgesic
Maintain bed rest for at least 1 week
Monitor urine output after catheter removal
Restrict fluid intake to 1 liter per day
instruction: Highlight the findings that require follow-up.

stem: A 67-year-old male has been admitted for a colonoscopy to identify the cause of per-rectal bleeding. It is most important to rule out the presence of which one of the following conditions before performing the procedure?
rationale: A colonoscopy cannot be performed in the setting of acute diverticulitis due to the risk of large intestinal perforation. Category: Gastrointestinal system, diagnostic tests, multiple choice question
options: Duodenal ulcer
Acute diverticulitis
Intestinal polyps
Intestinal vascular malformation
Colon cancer

stem: A 32-year-old female is admitted to the surgical ICU with a suspected diagnosis of intestinal obstruction. She has been complaining of worsening abdominal pain for the past 12 hours along with severe vomiting and abdominal distension. Her surgical record shows that she underwent an appendectomy last week. The client is in intense pain and begging for a painkiller. Which one of the following analgesics will be contraindicated in this scenario?
rationale: Opioid analgesics are contraindicated in clients with intestinal obstruction due to their negative effect on peristalsis. Key: Gastrointestinal system, Pharmacology, intensive care, multiple choice question
options: Acetaminophen
Methadone
Celecoxib
Meloxicam
Diclofenac sodium

stem: A 36-year-old male has been admitted to the Emergency Department with the complaint of severe epigastric pain, nausea and vomiting for the past 6 hours. He tells the admitting physician that he has been under severe stress at work recently and that he has been suffering from acidity for the past few weeks but it has been controlled so far with over the counter antacids. A diagnosis of acute gastritis is made and after receiving the appropriate therapy he is discharged. On the table below mark which self-care instructions the registered nurse would provide to this client.
rationale: Self-care instructions for a client with acute gastritis consist of the following: Decrease alcohol intake Decrease tea and coffee intake Avoid spicy foods Check the ASA content in over the counter drugs as they can aggravate the symptoms of acute gastritis Encourage smoking cessation Avoid exposure to radiation Category: Gastrointestinal system, preventive medicine, grid question Questions 202 to 205 are based on the scenario given below Category: Gastrointestinal system, emergency medicine, pediatrics, case study An 8-days-old male infant is brought to the Emergency Department by his parents with the complaint of excessive vomiting for the past one day. According to her the vomiting is projectile in nature, occurs immediately after feeding, and contains the milk that he has just had. On examination his vital signs are as follows: Heart rate: 220 bpm Respiratory rate: 50 bpm Temperature: 98.6 F SpO2: 98% The child is irritable with dry mucous membranes and when offered milk he is suckling eagerly though he vomits immediately. On abdominal examination, a hard mass is palpable in the left upper quadrant.
rows: ASA content in over the counter drugs is safe to use
Enroll in a program designed for smoking cessation

stem: In the scenario given above, identify the top 5 cues which require following up.
rationale: See rationale.
tokens: Contains the milk the child just had
instruction: Top 5 cues

stem: A diagnosis of pyloric stenosis is made after observing visible peristalsis before vomiting occurs and the diagnosis is confirmed via ultrasound. The registered nurse is most concerned that the client is at risk of __________________ due to _________________.
rationale: The immediate complication which needs to be corrected in a client with pyloric stenosis is dehydration and fluid imbalance.
options: Convulsions; inability to tolerate oral feeding
Dehydration; persistent vomiting
Growth retardation; inability to tolerate oral feeding
Esophageal bleeding; persistent, projectile vomiting
Metabolic acidosis; persistent vomiting

stem: On the list below, highlight which nursing orders should be followed immediately for this client to prevent the above complication.
rationale: The most urgent step in a client with persistent vomiting is to replace fluid and electrolytes. In the case of pyloric stenosis insert an NG tube for gastric decompression and check blood glucose levels to ensure the client is not suffering from hypoglycemia.
tokens: Obtain signed consent for a pyloromyotomy
instruction: Highlight the findings that require follow-up.

stem: In the table below mark the nursing interventions which are necessary for this client pre-operatively.
rationale: Pre-operative nursing care for a client with pyloric stenosis consists of the following: Check the client's weight daily Monitor NG tube and drainage Input and output charting Monitor electrolytes Maintain IV fluids with 0.9% sodium chloride and 5% glucose Reassure parents and explain the client’s condition to them
rows: Maintain IV fluids with 0.45% sodium chloride

stem: A first-time mother has brought her 2-month-old infant to the clinic with the complaint that he cries excessively at night. The mother has tried multiple ways to try and calm him down but he still cries throughout the night. On examination, the infant’s length and weight are in the 80th percentile for his age and there are no significant findings. He is responsive and playful and interested in toys held before him. On the list below, mark the self-care instructions which will be given to the mother by the registered nurse.
rationale: The scenario given above shows a case of infant colic. Self-care instructions are as follows: The infant’s crying spell will improve after 3 months of age There is no neurological damage A pacifier may be used to help soothe the baby Give a warm bath to the infant at night Teach the mother to give an abdominal massage Use a bottle with a collapsible bag as it helps decrease air entry into the stomach Category: Pediatrics, gastrointestinal system, multiple response question
options: The infant’s crying spell will improve after 3 months of age
After the crying phase ends make sure the infant gets a complete neurological checkup
Avoid using a pacifier as it can affect dentition
Give a warm bath to the infant at night
Teach the mother to give an abdominal massage
Do not use a bottle to feed the baby as it causes increased amounts of air to enter the stomach

stem: A 5-year-old female with chronic diarrhea and failure to thrive has been diagnosed with celiac disease after an extensive workup. Out of the dietary modifications given below which ones are indicated in this client? Mark them on the table below.
rationale: The following dietary modifications are indicated in celiac disease: Follow a gluten-free, high-protein diet Use rice flour and corn as a substitute for wheat flour Avoid using products made with wheat, oats, rye and barley Follow a low fat diet plan Take iron and folic acid supplements Take multivitamin and vitamin K supplements Category: Gastrointestinal system, pediatrics, multiple response question
rows: Avoid using products made with corn
Eliminate wheat and rye in the diet
Use oats and barley in place of wheat
Use rice flour as a substitute for wheat flour
Follow a low fat diet plan
Avoid folic acid supplements as they damage the intestinal lining

stem: A 32-year-old male with ulcerative colitis has undergone a total colectomy and ileostomy due to severe disease complications. On the list below, mark what self-care advice the client will receive in this case.
rationale: Self-care for an ileostomy is as follows: Avoid heavy lifting Resume all normal daily activities Stools in the ilesotomy bag should be liquid Report any abdominal pain, fever, vomiting or lack of output Drink at least 6 to 8 glasses of water per day to avoid dehydration Category: Gastrointestinal system, post-operative care, multiple response question
options: Avoid heavy lifting
Resume all normal daily activities
Stools in the ilesotomy bag should be semi-solid
Not having any output from the ileostomy for 24 hours is normal
Report any abdominal pain, fever or vomiting
Limit fluid intake to 1000 mL per day to avoid overloading the ileostomy bag

stem: A mother has brought her 1-year-old son to the pediatric clinic because she is concerned that his weight has consistently been in the 5th percentile since he was born. The infant is of Asian-American descent. On examination, he is standing with support, his height is within the 75th percentile and he is active and playful. He has achieved all the developmental milestones on time and he is fully immunized. Out of the statements below, which one is the most appropriate course of action for this scenario?
rationale: Asian-american children generally fall below the 5th percentile. Additionally, since the client has been in the 5th percentile since birth and is otherwise healthy his weight is not a concern and the mother should be reassured. Category: Gastrointestinal system, pediatrics, multiple choice question
options: Refer the mother to a dietitian
Call child protective services
Reassure the mother that the child is normal
Admit the infant under the diagnosis of failure to thrive
Schedule a follow up visit after 6 months

stem: A 10-month-old infant is brought to the Emergency Department by his parents due to severe projectile vomiting. What are the immediate nursing concerns for this client? Mark them on the list below.
rationale: The immediate nursing concerns for infants with vomiting are preventing aspiration and assessing dehydration and electrolyte imbalance. Category: Gastrointestinal system, pediatrics, emergency medicine, multiple response question
options: Identifying the cause
Preventing aspiration
Assessing the infant’s nutritional status
Assessing for dehydration
Checking blood glucose levels
Assesing for electrolyte imbalance

stem: In the table below, identify the top 4 cues from the scenario that will be of most concern to the registered nurse.
rationale: This client is experiencing a bowel obstruction which is indicated by the presence of severe abdominal pain, abdominal distension, vomiting, and complete constipation.

stem: A provisional diagnosis of intestinal obstruction is made and physician orders are given. On the list below, highlight which orders the registered nurse will carry out immediately.
rationale: The immediate nursing intervention in a client with intestinal obstruction should be fluid and electrolyte replacement, gastric decompression by insertion of an NG tube and confirmation of the diagnosis via ultrasound.
tokens: Get an urgent ultrasound of the abdomen
Send blood samples for baseline tests
Ambulate the client to improve peristalsis
instruction: Highlight the findings that require follow-up.

stem: In the table below, mark which nursing interventions are indicated for this client.
rationale: Nursing interventions in a client with intestinal obstruction include the following: Monitor serum electrolytes, particularly Potassium Administer intravenous fluids Keep the client nil per oral Assist in ambulation to promote peristalsis Measure abdominal circumference every 2 to 4 hours Monitor for edema Provide oral and nasal care Monitor NG tube output

Keep the client on bed rest
Encourage deep breathing to decrease pressure on lungs from distended abdomen

stem: On the second day of admission the nursing notes for this client show the following information:

stem: Once the client has been stabilized and his intestinal obstruction resolved, he is discharged. On the list below, mark which self-care instructions will be relevant for him.
rationale: Self-care instructions for a client with inflammatory bowel disease are as follows: Drink 2.5-3 liters of fluid daily Take salt and Gatorade during acute flares to replace electrolytes Take sitz baths to reduce anal irritation Monitor weight during acute flares Use a heating pad for abdominal pain but not during an acute exacerbation Report any fever or change in bowel habits Avoid raw fruits and vegetables only during acute exacerbations Lie down for 20 minutes after a meal to decrease peristalsis Questions 217 and 218 are based on the case study given below Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, case study A 33-year-old female has been admitted with a diagnosis of intestinal obstruction secondary to adhesions after uterine surgery. The registered nurse is inserting a nasogastric tube for gastric decompression.
options: Drink 2.5-3 liters of fluid daily
Avoid salt intake during acute flares
Take sitz baths to reduce anal irritation
Monitor weight during acute flares
Use a heating pad during acute exacerbations for abdominal pain
Report any fever or change in bowel habits
Avoid raw fruits and vegetables as much as possible
Take a walk after a meal to increase peristalsis

stem: Which one of the following measurement points will she use?
rationale: A nasogastric tube to be inserted into the stomach should be measured from the tip of the nose to the lobe of the ear to the xiphoid process.
options: A) From the tip of the nose to the angle of the jaw to the xiphoid process
B) From the lips to the lobe of the ear to the sternal body
C) From the tip of the nose to the ear lobe to the 12th rib
D) From the lips to the angle of the jaw to the xiphoid process
E) From the tip of the nose to the lobe of the ear to the xiphoid process

stem: In the table below mark the nursing interventions which will be required in this client.
rationale: Nursing care in a client with a nasogastric tube consists of the following: Check patency by instilling normal saline Note the amount and color of drainage from the tube Maintain suction at less than 25 mmHg Monitor skin turgor Assist with oral hygiene Monitor fluid and electrolyte balance Apply tape to the tube to keep it anchored in place
rows: Check patency by instilling normal saline
Note the amount and color of drainage from the tube
Maintain suction at greater than 30 mmHg
Auscultate for any decrease in breathing sounds
Do not tape the tube as it needs to move with peristalsis

stem: A 32-year-old male with Crohn’s disease has been started on Infliximab therapy after an acute exacerbation. Out of the following which blood tests need to be monitored for this client? Mark them on the list below.
rationale: Clients on immunomodulator drugs should have a complete blood count monthly to check for neutropenia and pancytopenia along with liver function tests. Category: Gastrointestinal system, pharmacology, multiple response question
options: Complete blood count
Renal function tests
Liver function tests
Pulmonary function tests
Serum electrolytes
Urinary sodium levels

stem: A 2-year-old male was admitted with a suspected intussusception due to the presence of inconsolable crying, right upper abdominal mass, and currant jelly stools. The diagnosis was confirmed via a barium enema and a hydrostatic reduction was done. Which one of the following most reliably indicates whether the procedure was successful?
rationale: Passage of normal stool is the most reliable indicator of successful reduction in an intussusception. Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, pediatrics, multiple choice question Questions 221 to 225 are based on the case study given below Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, pediatrics, case study An 8-week-old male infant is brought to the Emergency Department due to irritability, vomiting, excessive crying and passage of bloody stool. On examination, his vital signs are as follows: Temperature: 98.6 F Pulse: 140 bpm Respiratory rate: 58 bpm Blood pressure: 80/41 mmHg On abdominal examination, there is a palpable mass in the epigastrium and decreased bowel sounds.
options: Decrease in abdominal circumference
Passage of normal stool
Decrease in heart rate by 20 bpm and increase in systolic blood pressure by 20 mmHg
Normal bowel sounds
Decrease in crying

stem: In the scenario above, identify the top 4 cues that require following up.
rationale: See rationale.
instruction: Top 4 cues

stem: A provisional diagnosis of intussusception is made and confirmed via a barium enema and abdominal ultrasound. In the table below, mark the nursing interventions which are indicated in this client.
rationale: Nursing interventions in a client with intussusception consist of the following: Monitor vital signs Insert urinary catheter Insert a nasogastric tube Monitor for rectal bleeding Administer intravenous fluids
rows: Avoid inserting a nasogastric tube as it may cause intestinal rupture

stem: The nursing notes for this client show the following information
rationale: An increase in the pulse rate and a decrease in the blood pressure indicate that the client might be experiencing intestinal perforation, a possible complication of intussusception and a pediatric emergency.
options: Based on the above findings the registered nurse will be most concerned about which one of the following complications?
Intestinal obstruction
Pneumonia
Perforation
Dehydration
Sepsis

stem: In the list below, highlight which measures should be taken immediately for this client.
rationale: The treatment for an intestinal perforation is surgical resection, therefore the physician should be immediately notified and surgical consent obtained from the parents.
tokens: Obtain parental consent for emergency surgery
Send the client for an abdominal ultrasound
instruction: Highlight the findings that require follow-up.

stem: After surgical resection of the perforated intestine the client is admitted to the neonatal ICU. On the list below, select the nursing interventions which will be required for this client.
rationale: Post-operative care in a client with intussusception consists of the following: Administer intravenous fluids and electrolytes Monitor stool frequency, color and consistency Encourage breastfeeding Monitor vital signs
options: Administer intravenous fluids
Monitor stools
Encourage formula feeding to improve nutrient supply
Monitor vital signs
Place client on ventilator support for deep breathing
Avoid palpation of abdomen

stem: A 24-year-old female is being discharged after an appendectomy. In the table below mark the self-care instructions which are indicated for this client.
rationale: Self-care instructions after an appendectomy include the following: Wash the wound with soap and water Avoid bikini underwear so that there is no pressure on the wound Inspect the wound for any redness or drainage Resume normal activities over 2-3 weeks Avoid any heavy lifting Eat frequent, small meals Avoid strenuous exercise Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, post-operative care, grid question
rows: Do not wash the wound with soap and water to avoid irritation
Avoid bikini underwear so that there is no pressure on the wound
Inspect the wound for any redness or drainage

stem: The registered nurse in the pediatric ward is examining a newborn who was just delivered. He was born at 38 weeks of gestation via a spontaneous vaginal delivery. There were no prenatal or postnatal complications. During the examination, she notices that the client is passing a small amount of meconium through the urethral meatus while no meconium has been passed through the anus. She will suspect which one of the following disorders?
rationale: Failure to pass meconium through the anus and leakage through the vagina or urethral meatus should lead to suspicion for imperforate anus. Category: Surgery, pediatrics, gastrointestinal system, multiple choice question Questions 228 to 231 are based on the case study given below Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, geriatrics, case study A 68-year-old male is brought to the Emergency Department from a nursing home due to a small amount of rectal bleeding and two episodes of vomiting over the past 6 hours. His caretaker reports that he has also been refusing food for the past day and that he has a history of constipation on and off. The client himself has Alzheimer’s and is unable to give a proper history. On examination, his vital signs are as follows: Temperature: 100 F Pulse: 110 bpm Respiratory rate: 17 bpm Blood pressure: 130/90 mmHg On abdominal examination, the client reacts by pushing away the nurse’s hand when his left iliac fossa is palpated.
options: Hirschsprung disease
Intussusception
Meckel’s diverticulum
Imperforate anus
Volvulus

stem: Which disorder is this client most likely suffering from?
rationale: Left iliac fossa pain or discomfort along with low-grade fever in an elderly client are classic signs of diverticulitis. Rectal bleeding, nausea, and vomiting may also occur in cases of acute diverticulitis. The history of constipation further reinforces the diagnosis.
options: Appendicitis
Diverticulitis
Intestinal obstruction
Peritonitis
Gastroenteritis

stem: The diagnosis of diverticulitis is confirmed via a CT scan. In the table below, mark the nursing interventions indicated for this client.
rationale: Nursing interventions indicated for this client include the following: Keep the client nil per oral Administer intravenous fluids Monitor vital signs The client will be unable to mark his pain on the VAS due to cognitive decline as a result of Alzheimer’s Monitor complete blood count to check white blood cell levels
rows: Keep the client nil per oral
Ask the client to mark his pain level on the visual analog scale

stem: The nursing notes for this client on the 2nd day of admission show the following information:
rationale: A client with diverticulitis is at high risk for developing peritonitis and a white blood cell count higher than 20,000/mm3 along with a high neutrophil count confirms this differential.
options: Client is getting restless
Abdomen seems to be tender
Temperature 102 F
CBC shows a WBC count of 30,000/mm3 with a high neutrophil count
The registered nurse is concerned that the client has developed _____________________ as indicated by _____________________.
Appendicitis; high WBC count
Peritonitis; high WBC count and abdominal tenderness
Bowel ischemia; fever and abdominal tenderness
Volvulus; abdominal tenderness
Gastroenteritis; fever and abdominal tenderness

stem: The client is treated successfully with medical management and he is discharged after a week’s stay in the ICU. In the list below, highlight the care instructions which will be provided to the nursing home in order to prevent future episodes of diverticulitis.
rationale: Drink at least 2.5 liters of water per day Increased fruit and vegetable consumption Add small quantities of bran to foods Avoid weight lifting Avoid high fiber foods during episodes of acute diverticulitis as it increases irritation
steps: Drink at least 2.5 liters of water per day
Increased fruit and vegetable consumption
Avoid any weight lifting

stem: A 30-year-old overweight female visits her family physician with the complaint of bleeding per rectum for the past 6 months on and off. She has had constipation for over a year which has worsened in the past week along with the bleeding. The bleeding occurs with the passage of stool and consists of fresh blood. She also complains of itching in the anus. On rectal examination, she has multiple external hemorrhoids. In the table below, mark the self-care instructions given to this client.
rationale: Self-care instructions for this client will consist of the following: Drink at least 2 liters of water per day Avoid heavy lifting Exercise regularly Use mild laxatives if necessary Apply dibucaine ointment for symptomatic relief Take sitz baths Report excessive bleeding or any prolapse Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, grid question Questions 233 to 237 are based on the case study given below Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, case study A 75-year-old male with a 20 year history of diabetes mellitus visits the surgical OPD with the complaint of a swelling in the left inguinal region which has slowly increased in size over the past 8 months. There is no pain or redness associated with it. On examination, an inguinal hernia is palpable over the left inguinal region with a positive cough response and no signs of strangulation. The client is advised to wear a truss after manual reduction of the hernia.
rows: Drink at least 2 liters of water per day
Apply dibucaine ointment for symptomatic relief
A prolapsed hemorrhoid is not a cause for alarm

stem: In the table below mark the self-care advice which will be indicated for this client.
rationale: See rationale.
rows: Advise a sitz bath to relieve any pain over the hernia site

stem: 3 weeks later the client returns to the outpatient department with the complain of pain in the hernia which has been increasing steadily over the past week after he developed an upper respiratory tract infection which caused severe bouts of coughing. On examination, the hernia is now irreducible with erythema, tenderness and bowel sounds audible on auscultation. The client is advised a herniorraphy. Which one of the following factors in his history and examination would be a cause for delaying the surgery?
rationale: Any upper respiratory tract infections or allergies present pre-operatively should be treated before operating on the client as post-operative coughing and sneezing can damage the hernia repair.
options: Cough
Presence of bowel sounds
Rapid onset of symptoms
Advanced age
History of diabetes mellitus

stem: After treating his cough with anti-histamines the client is admitted for a herniorraphy which is successful. Postoperatively, his nursing notes show the following information:
rationale: Urinary retention may occur after an abdominal surgery and especially after a hernia repair during the first 8 hours. The client will complain of a sense of bladder fullness and urgency.
options: Temperature 98.6 F
Pulse 110 bpm
Respiratory rate 18 bpm
Fluid input 1000 mL per 12 hours
Fluid output 200 mL per 12 hours
Palpable urinary bladder
Client complaining of a sense of urinary urgency and lower abdominal pain
The registered nurse is concerned that the client is experiencing _____________________ as indicated by _____________________.
Acute kidney injury; decreased urine output
Urinary retention; decreased urine output and palpable bladder
Urinary tract infection; lower abdominal pain
Urethral stricture; decreased urine output
Urethral hemtoma; decreased urine output

stem: In the list below, highlight the nursing orders which must be carried out immediately for this client.
rationale: In case of urinary retention, inform the surgeon and initiate measures to assist in passage of urine.
tokens: Counsel the client for a suprapubic catheter
instruction: Highlight the findings that require follow-up.

stem: The client’s urinary retention is managed with the insertion of a urinary catheter. In the table below, mark the post-operative nursing interventions which will be indicated for this client.
rationale: See rationale.
rows: If sneezing is unavoidable sneeze with the mouth closed
Support the incision with both hands if coughing

stem: A 35-year-old female is being discharged after a stomach stapling procedure due to chronic obesity resulting in multiple medical complications. In the list below, select the self-care instructions which will be provided to this client.
rationale: The following self-care instructions are provided to the client after a restrictive bariatric surgery: Resume normal activities within 2 to 3 weeks Eat slowly and chew the food well Feeling bloated and nauseous after eating means the client has overeaten Avoid overeating as it can stretch the stomach pouch Take vitamin supplements regularly Avoid high-calorie liquids such as shakes and sodas Eat meals high in fiber to promote fullness for longer periods of time Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, multiple response question
options: Resume normal activities within 2 to 3 months
Eat slowly and chew the food well
Feeling bloated and nauseous after eating is normal after a restrictive bariatric surgery
Avoid overeating as it can stretch the stomach pouch
Take vitamin supplements regularly
Avoid high-calorie liquids such as shakes and sodas
Eat meals high in fat to promote fullness for longer periods of time

stem: A 32-year-old female has been admitted for a laparoscopic cholecystectomy for the treatment of cholecystitis. Her lab reports show the following information:
rationale: The treatment for a prolonged prothrombin time secondary to obstructive jaundice is administration of Vitamin K. Category: Gastrointestinal system, pharmacology, multiple choice question Questions 240 to 242 are based on the case study given below Category: Gastrointestinal system, surgery, post-operative care, case study
options: Raised conjugated bilirubin
Raised alkaline phosphatase
Raised GGT
Normal AST
Normal ALT
Prolonged prothrombin time
Normal INR
The client will require which one of the following medications before undergoing surgery?
Enoxaparin sodium
Vitamin K
Cholestyramine
Colestipol
N-acetylcysteine

stem: A 34-year-old female has been admitted to the surgical ward after a cholecystectomy which was converted from laparoscopic to open due to rupture of the gallbladder. A T-tube was inserted to assist in drainage of secretions. During the first 24 hours there is 400 ml of discharge from the T-tube which is bloody in character. On the list below, mark the appropriate action which should be taken by the registered nurse.
rationale: Bloody discharge of upto 500 ml is normal within the first 24 hours. After the first 24 hours the discharge decreases to 200 ml/day and turns green-brown.
options: Inform the physician immediately
Clamp the T-tube
Take no action
Administer a bolus of 500 ml N/S
Elevate the head of the bed

stem: In the table below mark the nursing interventions that are indicated for this client.
rationale: Nursing interventions post-operatively in a client with a T-tube insertion are as follows: Provide Demerol for pain management. Opiates are contraindicated as they can lead to biliary duct spasm Teach deep breathing techniques Position the client in semi-Fowler’s Monitor for T tube displacement Maintain a closed gravity T-tube drainage system Keep the drainage bag below the level of the T-tube Monitor stool color Do not irrigate, clamp or aspirate a T-tube
rows: Maintain an open gravity T-tube drainage system
Keep the drainage bag at the level of the T-tube

stem: After 5 days, the client is discharged from the hospital with the T-tube still in place. In the list below, select the self-care instructions which will be provided to this client.
rationale: Self-care instructions given to clients are as follows: Do not wear tight fitting clothes to avoid irritating the wound site Wear clothes which can be bleached as they may get stained with bile Avoid taking baths,, take showers instead to avoid wound infection Drain the bile bag daily at the same time Record the amount of drainage daily Resume normal activities gradually, as tolerated Heavy lifting may be resumed after 6 weeks
options: Do not wear tight fitting clothes
Wear clothes which can be bleached
Do not wear bikini underwear
Avoid taking showers, take baths instead
Drain the bile bag daily at the same time
Record the amount of drainage daily
Resume normal activities within 2 weeks
Heavy lifting may be resumed after 3 weeks

stem: A 34-year-old male visits the vaccination center to get vaccinated for Hepatitis B as he works as a caretaker in a nursing home. He received the first dose 4 months ago. Which one of the following plans of action is most suitable for this client?
rationale: If a dose of the Hepatitis B vaccine is missed, administer the followup dose as soon as possible. There is no need to start the schedule all over again. Category: Gastrointestinal system, vaccination, multiple choice question
options: Start the vaccination schedule again from the start
Administer the 2nd dose and schedule the third dose after 4 to 6 months
Administer the 2nd dose and schedule the third dose 1 month later
Administer the 2nd dose and schedule the third dose 1 week later
Administer Hepatitis B immunoglobulin along with the 2nd dose of the vaccine

stem: A 54-year-old male chronic alcoholic has been diagnosed with liver cirrhosis after signs of liver failure and confirmation with an abdominal ultrasound. Out of the following medications, which ones should this client be instructed to avoid? Mark them on the list below.
rationale: Acetaminophen, aspirin, amiodarone and amoxicillin-clavulanic acid are hepatotoxic drugs and should be avoided in a client with liver cirrhosis. Category: Gastrointestinal system, pharmacology, multiple response question
options: Acetaminophen
Paracetamol
Fentanyl
Aspirin
Amiodarone
Amoxicillin-clavulanic acid
Pregabalin

stem: Out of the following pediatric populations, which one will be at risk of developing gallstones?
rationale: Cholelithiasis is rare in the pediatric population except in children with sickle cell disease. Category: Gastrointestinal system, pediatrics, multiple choice question
options: Hemolytic anemia
Sickle cell disease
Turner syndrome
Down’s syndrome
Leukemia
